root_text.an3.117:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco pāṇātipātī hoti, adinnādāyī hoti, kāmesumicchācārī hoti, musāvādī hoti, pisuṇavāco hoti, pharusavāco hoti, samphappalāpī hoti.
root_text.an3.117:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco abhijjhālu hoti byāpannacitto.
root_text.an3.117:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco micchādiṭṭhiko hoti viparītadassano:
root_text.an3.117:3.3: ‘natthi dinnaṁ, natthi yiṭṭhaṁ, natthi hutaṁ, natthi sukatadukkaṭānaṁ, kammānaṁ phalaṁ vipāko, natthi ayaṁ loko, natthi paro loko, natthi mātā, natthi pitā, natthi sattā opapātikā, natthi loke samaṇabrāhmaṇā sammaggatā sammāpaṭipannā ye imañca lokaṁ parañca lokaṁ sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā pavedentī’ti.
root_text.an3.117:3.5: Sīlavipattihetu vā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjanti;
root_text.an3.117:3.6: cittavipattihetu vā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjanti;
root_text.an3.117:3.7: diṭṭhivipattihetu vā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.an3.117:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti, adinnādānā paṭivirato hoti, kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato hoti, musāvādā paṭivirato hoti, pisuṇāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti, pharusāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti, samphappalāpā paṭivirato hoti.
root_text.an3.117:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco anabhijjhālu hoti abyāpannacitto.
root_text.an3.117:7.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco sammādiṭṭhiko hoti aviparītadassano:
root_text.an3.117:7.3: ‘atthi dinnaṁ, atthi yiṭṭhaṁ, atthi hutaṁ, atthi sukatadukkaṭānaṁ kammānaṁ phalaṁ vipāko, atthi ayaṁ loko, atthi paro loko, atthi mātā, atthi pitā, atthi sattā opapātikā, atthi loke samaṇabrāhmaṇā sammaggatā sammāpaṭipannā ye imañca lokaṁ parañca lokaṁ sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā pavedentī’ti.
root_text.an3.117:7.5: Sīlasampadāhetu vā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti;
root_text.an3.117:7.6: cittasampadāhetu vā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti;
root_text.an3.117:7.7: diṭṭhisampadāhetu vā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

translation_text.an3.117:1.3: Failure in ethics, mind, and view.
translation_text.an3.117:1.4: And what is failure in ethics?
translation_text.an3.117:1.5: It’s when someone kills living creatures, steals, commits sexual misconduct, and uses speech that’s false, divisive, harsh, or nonsensical.
translation_text.an3.117:2.1: And what is failure in mind?
translation_text.an3.117:2.2: It’s when someone is covetous and malicious.
translation_text.an3.117:3.1: And what is failure in view?
translation_text.an3.117:3.2: It’s when someone has wrong view, a distorted perspective, such as:
translation_text.an3.117:3.3: ‘There’s no meaning in giving, sacrifice, or offerings. There’s no fruit or result of good and bad deeds. There’s no afterlife. There’s no such thing as mother and father, or beings that are reborn spontaneously. And there’s no ascetic or brahmin who is rightly comported and rightly practiced, and who describes the afterlife after realizing it with their own insight.’
translation_text.an3.117:3.5: Some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell because of failure in ethics,
translation_text.an3.117:4.3: Accomplishment in ethics, mind, and view.
translation_text.an3.117:5.1: And what is accomplishment in ethics?
translation_text.an3.117:5.2: It’s when someone doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, or use speech that’s false, divisive, harsh, or nonsensical.
translation_text.an3.117:5.3: This is called accomplishment in ethics.
translation_text.an3.117:6.1: And what is accomplishment in mind?
translation_text.an3.117:6.2: It’s when someone is content and kind-hearted.
translation_text.an3.117:6.3: This is called accomplishment in mind.
translation_text.an3.117:7.1: And what is accomplishment in view?
translation_text.an3.117:7.2: It’s when someone has right view, an undistorted perspective, such as:
translation_text.an3.117:7.3: ‘There is meaning in giving, sacrifice, and offerings. There are fruits and results of good and bad deeds. There is an afterlife. There are such things as mother and father, and beings that are reborn spontaneously. And there are ascetics and brahmins who are rightly comported and rightly practiced, and who describe the afterlife after realizing it with their own insight.’
translation_text.an3.117:7.4: This is called accomplishment in view.
translation_text.an3.117:7.5: Some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm because of accomplishment in ethics,
